p1|POB.summary: Employment status last week (open-ended follow-up on work activities).

p2|POB.summary: Additional work activities beyond main employment last week (follow-up to p1).

p3|POB.summary: Employment arrangement for main job (self-employed, employee, etc.).

p6|POB.summary: Primary work activity performed last week.

p9|POB.summary: Access to healthcare benefits through primary employment.

p11|POB.summary: Father's social class when respondent was 18 years old.

p12|POB.summary: Children's current or expected social class.

p13|POB.summary: Self-perceived resilience in facing life's adversities (strength scale).

p14|POB.summary: Perceived influence of national economic situation on personal well-being.

p15|POB.summary: Perceived personal control over economic success this year and next.

p17_1|POB.summary: Satisfaction with available time for leisure activities (0-10 scale).

p19_1|POB.summary: First-mentioned priority household expense when money is scarce.

p20_1|POB.summary: First-mentioned descriptor of well-being from provided list.

p21_2|POB.summary: Perceived ability of most Mexicans to access quality public health services.

p22|POB.summary: Respondent's economic situation compared to parents' situation at age 18.

p23|POB.summary: Expected future economic situation of children/grandchildren compared to respondent's current situation.

p24|POB.summary: Perceived change in young people's opportunities to achieve upward mobility compared to their parents.

p29_1|POB.summary: First-mentioned government spending priority when resources are limited.

p30|POB.summary: Preferred partner for collaboration in poverty reduction efforts.

p31|POB.summary: Respondent's opinion on what is needed to end poverty.

p32|POB.summary: Belief in whether Mexico has first and second-class citizens or all are equal.

p33|POB.summary: Respondent's perception of how society treats them (first or second-class).

p34|POB.summary: Opinion on how citizens can best contribute to reducing social problems.

p35|POB.summary: Agreement with statement that those born poor almost always die poor.

p36|POB.summary: Agreement with statement that education is necessary but no longer guarantees good employment.

p37|POB.summary: Belief in whether discrimination against poor people exists in Mexico.

p39|POB.summary: Opinion on who is the main enemy of the poor.

p40|POB.summary: Opinion on who is primarily responsible for social problems.

p41|POB.summary: Most important principle for defining social policies.

p42|POB.summary: Preferred funding mechanism for social policies.

p43|POB.summary: Perceived effectiveness of government social programs in helping people escape poverty.

p44|POB.summary: Agreement with statement about social policy approach (multiple choice).

p46_1|POB.summary: Perceived willingness of neighbors to help (community social capital).

p47|POB.summary: Total number of rooms/bedrooms in household.

p48|POB.summary: Number of complete bathrooms with shower and toilet in household.

p49|POB.summary: Availability of functioning shower in household bathrooms.

p51|POB.summary: Predominant flooring material in household (dirt, cement, or finished).

p52|POB.summary: Number of personal automobiles owned by household.

p53|POB.summary: Number of functioning color televisions in household.

p54|POB.summary: Number of functioning personal computers in household.

p55|POB.summary: Availability of gas or electric stove in household.

p56|POB.summary: Highest completed education level of primary household income earner.
